commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=08cb9f68a9ba1100fe139453a47d96c0a1661fd2)
commands.review_summary:
The PR removes `shouldRethrowAbort`, changes `runFallbackCandidate` AbortError fallback handling, and adds model-fallback tests for timeout aborts and provider-side AbortError fallback.

PR surface: Source -2, Tests +27. Total +25 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. The reported provider-side abort issue is source-reproducible from the fallback and non-direct failure-routing paths, and the introduced cancellation regression is also source-reproducible from `createUserAbortError`, `isTerminalAbort`, and the fallback loop.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed: fix the cancellation regression and provide current-head real behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not repair this PR while proof remains insufficient.

target.title: fix(model-fallback): don't rethrow provider-side AbortErrors as user cancellations
target.body:
## Problem

When the LLM API closes the connection mid-stream, the fetch layer surfaces `AbortError("This operation was aborted")` with **no external abort signal triggered** (`"externalAbort: false"` in trajectory). The existing guard `shouldRethrowAbort()` returned `false` for these errors (because `isTimeoutError` matched the "operation was aborted" message), so they fell through to the fallback loop but were never retried — the error propagated up and produced `SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN` in group/channel sessions, permanently silencing the topic.

**Reproduction path (source inspection + confirmed by real trajectory):**

1. Provider drops connection → `AbortError("This operation was aborted")`, `"promptErrorSource: "prompt"`, `"externalAbort: false"`
2. `runFallbackCandidate`: `shouldRethrowAbort` = false (isTimeoutError matches) → error not rethrown, not retried → `return { ok: false, error }`
3. `agent-runner-execution.ts` catch: `isGenericRunnerFailure: true`
4. `resolveExternalRunFailureTextForConversation`: group/channel session + generic failure → `SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN`
5. User receives **zero visible response**; every subsequent message in the same topic repeats the same path → topic is permanently silenced

## Fix

Replace the guard in `runFallbackCandidate` with a direct check: only rethrow `AbortError` when the external abort signal is actually set (user/gateway cancellation). Provider-side `AbortError`s without an external signal now fall through to the next fallback candidate.

```typescript
// Before:
if (shouldRethrowAbort(err) && !normalizedFailover) {
  throw err;
}

// After:
if (isFallbackAbortError(err) && !params.abortSignal?.aborted && !normalizedFailover) {
  throw err;
}
```

This also removes the unused `shouldRethrowAbort` helper and `isTimeoutError` import.

## Scope

- **2 files changed**, +31 / -6
- `model-fallback.ts`: guard condition change (core fix)
- `model-fallback.test.ts`: updated existing user-abort test + added provider-side abort regression test

## What this does NOT change

- `agent-runner-execution.ts` failure routing (SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN for generic group failures) — this is a separate concern that can be addressed in a follow-up if needed
- `compact.ts` abort signal forwarding
- No config, plugin, or gateway protocol changes

## Before-fix behavior proof (redacted)

Two trajectory records from the affected installation:

**Event 1 — 2026-06-06T02:14:27Z**
```json
{ "type": "trace.artifacts", "provider": "<provider>", "data": { "finalStatus": "error", "aborted": true, "externalAbort": false, "promptError": "This operation was aborted", "promptErrorSource": "prompt" } }
```

**Event 2 — 2026-06-06T09:02:28Z** (same session — confirms repeat-silence pattern)
```json
{ "type": "trace.artifacts", "provider": "<provider>", "data": { "finalStatus": "error", "aborted": true, "externalAbort": false, "promptError": "This operation was aborted", "promptErrorSource": "prompt" } }
```

Both: `externalAbort: false` → no external signal fired → `dispatch complete replies=0` in gateway logs.

## Tests

78/78 model-fallback tests pass. New regression test `"falls back when AbortError comes from the LLM provider (no external signal)"` verifies that provider-side AbortError enters the fallback chain instead of being rethrown.
target.author: shengting

target.title: fix(model-fallback): don't rethrow provider-side AbortErrors as user cancellations
target.body:
## Problem

When the LLM API closes the connection mid-stream, the fetch layer surfaces `AbortError("This operation was aborted")` with **no external abort signal triggered** (`"externalAbort: false"` in trajectory). The existing guard `shouldRethrowAbort()` returned `false` for these errors (because `isTimeoutError` matched the "operation was aborted" message), so they fell through to the fallback loop but were never retried — the error propagated up and produced `SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN` in group/channel sessions, permanently silencing the topic.

**Reproduction path (source inspection + confirmed by real trajectory):**

1. Provider drops connection → `AbortError("This operation was aborted")`, `"promptErrorSource: "prompt"`, `"externalAbort: false"`
2. `runFallbackCandidate`: `shouldRethrowAbort` = false (isTimeoutError matches) → error not rethrown, not retried → `return { ok: false, error }`
3. `agent-runner-execution.ts` catch: `isGenericRunnerFailure: true`
4. `resolveExternalRunFailureTextForConversation`: group/channel session + generic failure → `SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN`
5. User receives **zero visible response**; every subsequent message in the same topic repeats the same path → topic is permanently silenced

## Fix

Replace the guard in `runFallbackCandidate` with a direct check: only rethrow `AbortError` when the external abort signal is actually set (user/gateway cancellation). Provider-side `AbortError`s without an external signal now fall through to the next fallback candidate.

```typescript
// Before:

  throw err;
}

// After:
if (isFallbackAbortError(err) && !params.abortSignal?.aborted && !normalizedFailover) {
  throw err;
}
```

This also removes the unused `shouldRethrowAbort` helper and `isTimeoutError` import.

## Scope

- **2 files changed**, +31 / -6
- `model-fallback.ts`: guard condition change (core fix)
- `model-fallback.test.ts`: updated existing user-abort test + added provider-side abort regression test

## What this does NOT change

- `agent-runner-execution.ts` failure routing (SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN for generic group failures) — this is a separate concern that can be addressed in a follow-up if needed
- `compact.ts` abort signal forwarding
- No config, plugin, or gateway protocol changes

## Before-fix behavior proof (redacted)

Two trajectory records from the affected installation:

**Event 1 — 2026-06-06T02:14:27Z**
```json
{ "type": "trace.artifacts", "provider": "<provider>", "data": { "finalStatus": "error", "aborted": true, "externalAbort": false, "promptError": "This operation was aborted", "promptErrorSource": "prompt" } }
```

**Event 2 — 2026-06-06T09:02:28Z** (same session — confirms repeat-silence pattern)
```json
{ "type": "trace.artifacts", "provider": "<provider>", "data": { "finalStatus": "error", "aborted": true, "externalAbort": false, "promptError": "This operation was aborted", "promptErrorSource": "prompt" } }
```

Both: `externalAbort: false` → no external signal fired → `dispatch complete replies=0` in gateway logs.

## Tests

78/78 model-fallback tests pass. New regression test `"falls back when AbortError comes from the LLM provider (no external signal)"` verifies that provider-side AbortError enters the fallback chain instead of being rethrown.

```json
{ "type": "trace.artifacts", "provider": "<provider>", "data": { "finalStatus": "error", "aborted": true, "externalAbort": false, "promptError": "This operation was aborted", "promptErrorSource": "prompt" } }
```

**Event 2 — 2026-06-06T09:02:28Z** (same session — confirms repeat-silence pattern)
```json
{ "type": "trace.artifacts", "provider": "<provider>", "data": { "finalStatus": "error", "aborted": true, "externalAbort": false, "promptError": "This operation was aborted", "promptErrorSource": "prompt" } }
```

Both: `externalAbort: false` → no external signal fired → `dispatch complete replies=0` in gateway logs.

## Tests

78/78 model-fallback tests pass. New regression test `"falls back when AbortError comes from the LLM provider (no external signal)"` verifies that provider-side AbortError enters the fallback chain instead of being rethrown.

commands.author: shengting

target.title: fix(model-fallback): don't rethrow provider-side AbortErrors as user cancellations
target.body:
## Problem

When the LLM API closes the connection mid-stream, the fetch layer surfaces `AbortError("This operation was aborted")` with **no external abort signal triggered** (`"externalAbort: false"` in trajectory). The existing guard `shouldRethrowAbort()` returned `false` for these errors (because `isTimeoutError` matched the "operation was aborted" message), so they fell through to the fallback loop but were never retried — the error propagated up and produced `SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN` in group/channel sessions, permanently silencing the topic.

**Reproduction path (source inspection + confirmed by real trajectory):**

1. Provider drops connection → `AbortError("This operation was aborted")`, `"promptErrorSource: "prompt"`, `"externalAbort: false"`
2. `runFallbackCandidate`: `shouldRethrowAbort` = false (isTimeoutError matches) → error not rethrown, not retried → `return { ok: false, error }`
3. `agent-runner-execution.ts` catch: `isGenericRunnerFailure: true`
4. `resolveExternalRunFailureTextForConversation`: group/channel session + generic failure → `SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN`
5. User receives **zero visible response**; every subsequent message in the same topic repeats the same path → topic is permanently silenced

## Fix

Replace the guard in `runFallbackCandidate` with a direct check: only rethrow `AbortError` when the external abort signal is actually set (user/gateway cancellation). Provider-side `AbortError`s without an external signal now fall through to the next fallback candidate.

```typescript
// Before:
if (shouldRethrowAbort(err) && !normalizedFailover) {
  throw err;
}

// After:
if (isFallbackAbortError(err) && !params.abortSignal?.aborted && !normalizedFailover) {
  throw err;
}
```

This also removes the unused `shouldRethrowAbort` helper and `isTimeoutError` import.

## Scope

- **2 files changed**, +31 / -6
- `model-fallback.ts`: guard condition change (core fix)
- `model-fallback.test.ts`: updated existing user-abort test + added provider-side abort regression test

## What this does NOT change

- `agent-runner-execution.ts` failure routing (SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN for generic group failures) — this is a separate concern that can be addressed in a follow-up if needed
- `compact.ts` abort signal forwarding
- No config, plugin, or gateway protocol changes

## Before-fix behavior proof (redacted)

Two trajectory records from the affected installation:

**Event 1 — 2026-06-06T02:14:27Z**
```json
{ "type": "trace.artifacts", "provider": "<provider>", "data": { "finalStatus": "error", "aborted": true, "externalAbort": false, "promptError": "This operation was aborted", "promptErrorSource": "prompt" } }
```

**Event 2 — 2026-06-06T09:02:28Z** (same session — confirms repeat-silence pattern)
```json
{ "type": "trace.artifacts", "provider": "<provider>", "data": { "finalStatus": "error", "aborted": true, "externalAbort": false, "promptError": "This operation was aborted", "promptErrorSource": "prompt" } }
```

Both: `externalAbort: false` → no external signal fired → `dispatch complete replies=0` in gateway logs.

## Tests

78/78 model-fallback tests pass. New regression test `"falls back when AbortError comes from the LLM provider (no external signal)"` verifies that provider-side AbortError enters the fallback chain instead of being rethrown.
